'''Luj City''' was located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Novor 23]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]. It was an important financial center of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and also included the seedy [[East Side]] district. The latter was the site of a confrontation that took place around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] and pitted the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Kassar Kosciusko]] against five members of the [[Loag]] order of hired killers.

==Description==
Luj City was the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|settlement]] of [[Novor 23]], a busy [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with large metropolitan centers that was situated in the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]<ref name="Sisar" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="OuterRim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Members of the planet's native [[Ry'coz]] [[Species/Legends|species]] cooperated with immigrated [[Alien/Legends|alien]] populations in order to create a bustling economy, and Luj City served as one of the most important financial centers of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="Sisar" /> The city shared its name with the [[Tamarin sector/Legends|Tamarin sector's]] [[Luj system/Legends|Luj system]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> and contained a seedy district known as [[East Side]].<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
Approximately [[20 BBY/Legends|twenty years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], different parties contracted the [[Tarro]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Kassar Kosciusko]] and five members of the [[Loag]]<ref name="CRO" />—an ancient order of assassins originating on the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]] planet [[Merisee/Legends|Merisee]]<ref name="Planets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref>—to [[Death/Legends|eliminate]] the same target. The two parties of killers had a confrontation in Luj City's East Side from which Kosciusko, albeit heavily scarred, emerged as the sole survivor.<ref name="CRO" /> The same district was also the site of a brawl between a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] enforcer and the [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Milacass]],<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> which took place at some point by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]<ref name="2 ABY">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> and in which the latter lost several [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Luj City was created by [[Craig Robert Carey]] and [[J. Michael Denbo]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" />
